en.extract:
The Cook reed warbler (Acrocephalus kerearako) or Cook Islands reed warbler, is a species of Old World warbler in the family Acrocephalidae. It is native to the southeastern Cook Islands. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical dry forests, swamps, and rural gardens. It is threatened by habitat loss.


Subspecies
A. k. kaoko Holyoak, 1974 – Mitiaro
A. k. kerearako Holyoak, 1974 – Mangaia


References

The '''Cook reed warbler''' ('''''Acrocephalus kerearako''''') or '''Cook Islands reed warbler''', is a species of [[Old World warbler]] in the family [[Acrocephalidae]]. It is native to the southeastern [[Cook Islands]]. Its natural [[habitat]]s are subtropical or tropical dry [[forest]]s, [[swamp]]s, and rural gardens. It is threatened by [[habitat loss]].

== Subspecies ==
* ''A. k. kaoko'' <small>Holyoak, 1974</small> – [[Mitiaro]]
* ''A. k. kerearako'' <small>Holyoak, 1974</small> – [[Mangaia]]
